title: Providing for congressional disapproval under chapter 8 of title 5, United States Code, of the rule submitted by the Environmental Protection Agency relating to "Trichloroethylene (TCE); Regulation Under the Toxic Substances Control Act (TSCA)".
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
plain_summary: This bill would cancel a new EPA rule about a chemical called trichloroethylene or TCE. The EPA rule that was made in December 2024 stops companies from making, importing, or selling TCE for any use. The rule also stops businesses from using TCE in their work. If Congress passes this bill, the EPA rule would be thrown out and have no power. This means TCE could still be made, sold, and used like before.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Joint Resolution
sections.text: Congress does not approve of a final rule that the Environmental Protection Agency made about a chemical called Trichloroethylene (a cleaning chemical also known as TCE). This rule was created under the Toxic Substances Control Act (a law that controls dangerous chemicals). The EPA published this rule in the Federal Register on December seventeenth, two thousand twenty four. Because Congress disapproves of this rule, it will have no power and cannot be enforced.
